data.headline: Home Remodeling in Westport, Connecticut
data.subheadline: Serving Westport homeowners from our Orange, CT headquarters — full-service remodeling for kitchens, bathrooms, basements, and flooring.

data.title: Home Remodeling in Westport, CT
data.content:
BuiltWell CT serves Westport, Connecticut from our headquarters at 206A Boston Post Road in Orange — about 30 minutes from our Orange office. We handle every project in-house with our own crews, never subcontracting your work to unknown teams.

Whether you're planning a kitchen renovation, a bathroom update, a finished basement, or new flooring, we bring the same approach to every Westport project: a thorough initial assessment, a clear written proposal with specific line items, and daily communication throughout construction.

We hold CT Home Improvement Contractor License #0668405 and carry full liability and workers' compensation insurance.

data.title: Recent Fairfield County Projects

items.title: Kitchen Remodeling in Westport
items.description:
A Westport homeowner needed a complete kitchen renovation — removing a wall to create an open concept layout, installing custom shaker cabinetry, quartz countertops, and a professional-grade range. We managed all permits and inspections.

"The level of communication throughout the project was exceptional. Every detail was accounted for." — Sarah M., Westport

items.title: Bathroom Suite in Greenwich
items.description:
A Greenwich couple wanted to transform their dated master bathroom into a spa-inspired retreat. We installed a walk-in shower with frameless glass, heated porcelain floors, a freestanding soaking tub, and custom double vanity with quartz top.

"It feels like a five-star hotel every morning. The tile work is absolutely flawless." — The Hartley Family, Greenwich

items.title: Whole-Home Remodeling in Fairfield
items.description:
A Fairfield family purchased a 1960s colonial and needed a full renovation — two bathrooms, kitchen, hardwood flooring throughout, and a finished basement for a home office. We managed the entire project under a single contract.

"One contractor for everything. No coordination headaches. The house feels brand new." — The Chen Family, Fairfield

data.title: Remodeling in Westport, Connecticut
data.content:
Westport's residential neighborhoods feature a distinctive mix of colonials, mid-century moderns, and contemporary homes built from the 1950s through the 2000s. Many homes in Compo Beach, Greens Farms, and Saugatuck Shores areas present unique coastal considerations alongside standard renovation needs — dated kitchens, original bathrooms, and aging mechanical systems.

Westport is a vibrant Fairfield County community known for its arts scene, excellent schools, and waterfront living. Homeowners here invest thoughtfully in their properties and expect clear communication throughout the renovation process. The Westport Building Department requires detailed plans for most renovation projects, and coastal zone regulations apply to many waterfront properties. We navigate these requirements routinely.

Westport is about 30 minutes from our Orange office. That proximity means faster response times, no travel delays, and direct familiarity with Westport's housing stock and building department requirements. When you call us about a project in Westport, you're reaching a team that already knows the conditions your home is likely to present.

data.title: Westport Building Department & Permits
data.content:
Kitchen and bathroom remodeling in Westport requires permits from the Westport Building Department for any structural, electrical, or plumbing work. Most renovation projects involve all three.

We handle all permit applications, schedule all required inspections, and coordinate the sequencing so the permit process doesn't delay your construction timeline. You never need to visit town hall or coordinate with inspectors yourself — that's included in every project scope.

Being based in Orange means we're about 30 minutes from our Orange office and can respond quickly to any inspection questions or follow-ups the Westport Building Department requires.

data.title: Our Remodeling Process
data.subtitle: Every project follows the same five-step process. This structure keeps projects on schedule, on budget, and clearly communicated throughout.

steps.title: Consultation
steps.description: We visit your home or connect via Google Meet or Zoom to discuss your goals, assess the space, and answer your questions. No charge. No obligation. We look at the layout, note the existing electrical and plumbing conditions, and get a clear picture of what you want to accomplish.

steps.title: Planning
steps.description: You receive a clear written proposal covering exactly what's included, how long it will take, and what it costs. Line items are specific. We break out cabinetry, countertops, tile, electrical, plumbing, flooring, and permits separately so you understand exactly where the budget is going.

steps.title: Selections
steps.description: We guide you through material choices with options at different price points. We communicate lead times clearly so selections are made on schedule before construction begins. If a material has a long lead time, we flag that immediately.

steps.title: Build
steps.description: Construction begins on the agreed schedule. You receive daily updates on progress, a clean job site at the end of every workday, and crews who arrive when scheduled. If something unexpected comes up behind a wall, we contact you that day and present your options.

steps.title: Walkthrough
steps.description: We walk through the finished project together. We check every cabinet, every drawer, every tile, every light fixture. If anything needs attention, we address it before calling the project complete. Your written acceptance at the final walkthrough is the last step.

data.headline: Our Orange Office

data.title: Serving Two Counties from Orange
data.subtitle: From our Orange headquarters, we serve homeowners throughout Fairfield and New Haven Counties with dedicated teams in both regions.

items.question: Do you serve Westport, CT?
items.answer: Yes. We serve Westport and all of Fairfield County from our office in Orange, CT — about 30 minutes from our Orange office. Call (203) 466-9148 to schedule a free consultation.

items.question: Do I need a permit for remodeling in Westport?
items.answer: Most remodeling projects in Westport require permits from the Westport Building Department for structural, electrical, and plumbing work. We handle all permit applications and coordinate every required inspection as part of the project scope.

items.question: How do I schedule a consultation?
items.answer: Call (203) 466-9148 or fill out the contact form on this page. We offer both in-person and remote consultations via Google Meet. In-person visits for Westport projects are easy to schedule since we're based about 30 minutes from our Orange office. No charge, no obligation.

items.question: Are you licensed and insured?
items.answer: Yes. BuiltWell CT holds Connecticut Home Improvement Contractor license #0668405. We carry full general liability insurance and workers' compensation coverage. Copies available upon request.

items.question: How long does a typical remodeling project take?
items.answer: Timelines vary by scope. Bathroom remodels typically take 3–6 weeks, kitchen remodels 6–12 weeks, and basement finishing 4–8 weeks. We provide a detailed schedule in every written proposal.

items.question: What types of homes do you work on in Westport?
items.answer: Westport's housing stock is primarily colonials, mid-century moderns, and contemporary homes built between the 1950s through 2000s. We're familiar with the construction conditions common in these homes and account for them in every proposal.

items.question: Do you handle permits and inspections?
items.answer: Yes. Permit coordination is included in every project scope. We handle all building, electrical, and plumbing permit applications and schedule every required inspection with the Westport Building Department.

items.question: What does your proposal include?
items.answer: Every BuiltWell proposal is a detailed, line-item document that breaks out costs for each component: cabinetry, countertops, tile, plumbing, electrical, flooring, permits, and labor. No hidden fees.

items.question: Do you offer financing?
items.answer: Yes. We partner with GreenSky to offer flexible financing options. Plans include deferred interest and fixed monthly payments. We walk you through available plans during your consultation.

items.question: What happens if something unexpected comes up during construction?
items.answer: If we discover something behind a wall or under a floor — outdated wiring, water damage, code violations — we contact you that same day, explain what we found, present options with costs, and don't proceed until you approve in writing.
